statement: `llm_requests` should remain the immutable attempt ledger, while `process_request_targets` should be redesigned into first-class current target truth.
rationale: The current code already appends retries as new request rows and uses `process_request_targets` as a per-target rollup, so the redesign aligns with existing behavior rather than fighting it.
keyAssumptions: Current hot-path consumers do not need raw attempt history to compute stage progress.
We are willing to keep attempt history as a separate forensic lane.
validationCriteria: Core run/window progress can be computed from target truth plus artifact rows.
Diagnostics surfaces can expose attempt history separately without losing information.
critiquePoints: Some debugging flows may still rely on the existing blended target snapshot.
questions: Should `process_request_targets` become authoritative state or remain derived but explicit?
